summary: Matched and linked src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c by switching itlinkarrow.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, tightening function signatures and symbol names, reordering/reshaping code to match compiler output, and updating GALE01 symbol metadata for now-local literals, jump tables, and sdata2 constants. The decomp.dev report showed +1144 matched-code bytes, +8904 linked-code bytes, +48 matched-data bytes, and +336 linked-data bytes; it also identified itLinkArrow_802A850C as newly 100% matched and noted a small regression in unmatched vi0501::un_8031D9F8 caused by the related sdata2 ordering work.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c is built as Object(Matching, ...) instead of Object(NonMatching, ...).
Renamed the symbol and declaration at 0x802A81C4 from fn_802A81C4 to itLinkArrow_802A81C4 in symbols.txt, itlinkarrow.c, itlinkarrow.h, and an inline-related comment.

Refactored itLinkArrow_802A81C4 variable names and lookup-table indexing: it now indexes from it_803F6A84 using lookup_table[0] and lookup_table[8] rather than casting around it_803F6A28 with larger offsets.
Moved the implementation of it_802A83E0 later in itlinkarrow.c without materially changing its spawn-item initialization behavior, indicating function order/layout was part of matching/linking.
Changed itLinkArrow_802A850C return type from void to bool and returned the result of it_8026EA20 in the branch that previously called it without returning its value.
Moved itLinkArrow_Logic98_Clanked below itLinkArrow_Logic98_HitShield and preserved its behavior of calling it_802A8330 and returning true, suggesting ordering or local layout mattered.
Changed itLinkarrow_UnkMotion4_Anim's final return from a zero local to return false.
Added a small static helper sdata2_ordering in itlinkarrow.c with a volatile 0.0f local, apparently to influence floating-constant emission/order.

Added static f32 getScale() in vi0501.c returning 1.4285715f * un_804D6F80 and used it in un_8031D9F8; this made vi0501 .sdata2 reach 100% according to the bot report but slightly regressed the unmatched function score.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to convert several itlinkarrow-adjacent data labels/constants from global it_* or generic lbl_/jumptable_* names to local compiler-style names such as @263, @265, @299, @741, and ...data.0.
smart_moves: Used function placement/reordering rather than behavioral rewrites where the spawn helper it_802A83E0 and Clanked callback needed to land in the correct object layout.
Fixed the signature of itLinkArrow_802A850C to bool so the wrapper can directly return it_8026EA20's result, eliminating a one-byte mismatch reported as going from 99.95% to 100%.
Replaced fragile pointer arithmetic against it_803F6A28 with a clearer lookup-table base at it_803F6A84 while preserving the same angle-randomization calculation.
Introduced tiny static helpers for floating constants, including volatile-zero and getScale patterns, to control sdata2 constant ordering and emission.
Updated symbol-map scope and names for literals, jump tables, and sdata2 constants after matching, preventing misleading global it_* names for local compiler-generated objects.
decomp_lessons: For Melee item files, final matching may depend on function order and static/local data ordering even when behavior is already close.
When a callee returns bool, a caller declared void can hide a small mismatch; checking real return usage can be enough to close a near-100% function.
Compiler-style local labels in symbols.txt, such as @NNN and ...data.N, should replace placeholder global data names once a unit's local data layout is understood.
A small helper function can be a practical tactic to isolate or force emission of floating constants into the desired sdata2 order.
Bot reports are useful for detecting cross-unit data side effects: matching vi0501 .sdata2 improved, but un_8031D9F8 had a one-byte regression while still unmatched.
Do not infer extensive intent from this PR body: the PR had no descriptive body and no human review comments in the slice; evidence comes mainly from the diff and decomp.dev report.
naming_conventions: Use item-specific camel-case prefixes for identified functions, e.g. itLinkArrow_802A81C4 instead of generic fn_802A81C4.
Keep unknown but address-anchored item functions as it_802A.... when no stronger semantic name is established.
Use compiler/local-style symbol names like @263, @299, @741, and ...data.0 for local strings, jump tables, and data labels rather than promoting them to global it_* names.
Header declarations should mirror corrected source signatures, including bool return types for functions that return boolean results.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed an almost-matching function by returning it_8026EA20 directly from itLinkArrow_802A850C.
Used local helper getScale in vi0501.c to affect constant placement for 1.4285715f and complete vi0501 .sdata2 matching.
Added sdata2_ordering with a volatile f32 zero to influence floating constant ordering in itlinkarrow.c.
Adjusted source function ordering to match object layout, including moving it_802A83E0 and itLinkArrow_Logic98_Clanked.
Rebased angle lookup calculations on it_803F6A84 to match array/data access patterns.
Updated symbols.txt scope and labels for local .data, .sdata, and .sdata2 objects after matching/linking exposed the correct local symbol identities.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 71.51% (+0.03%, +1144 bytes), linked code 42.78% (+0.23%, +8904 bytes), matched data 41.44% (+48 bytes), linked data 35.06% (+336 bytes).
decomp.dev bot reported 2 new matches: main/melee/vi/vi0501 .sdata2 reached 100.00%, and main/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow itLinkArrow_802A850C reached 100.00%.
decomp.dev bot reported 1 regression in an unmatched item: main/melee/vi/vi0501 un_8031D9F8 changed from 98.83% to 98.76%.
follow_up_queries: Search for other item files with void declarations around bool-returning collision helpers like it_8026EA20.
Look for remaining generic fn_ symbols in item files that can be safely renamed to item-specific prefixes after matching.
Review other near-matching item units for function-order-only mismatches and local data symbol scope cleanup.
Investigate whether vi0501::un_8031D9F8 can recover the one-byte unmatched regression while preserving the now-100% .sdata2 layout.
Find additional uses of volatile-zero or tiny static helper patterns for sdata2 ordering across the project.
